spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries surface (paintTime, presentationTime, LCP); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: implementation-defined.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener, input); controls include "50 ms burst"; "100 ms burst"; "200 ms burst" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5162859838046208.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on loaf-presentation-inspector
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring loaf-presentation-inspector back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>. See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: loaf-presentation-inspector covers the happy path of Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
